review_text: The staff were extremely friendly and helpful and the park was clean and well maintained.
Absolutely loved it staff were friendly and helpful 😀

we have our own C/van don't need a cabin very disappointed with the state of showers etc i repoted abot the toilet seat nearly off and pne cubical didn't have a lock i've been here 10 days and nothings been done to fix them

Excellent location
Good amenities
Clean and tidy

Nice open spaces.Sparkling pool, kids play room and Adult lounge with good tv selection .
Good roads ,clean facilities.
Good quality ensuites with good water pressure.
5 minutes to town .
Walk to the beach.
Happily extended my stay.

A nice park with beach access and clean and tidy surroundings. Main amenities blocks were clean and tidy but are due for an upgrade. Bbq areas are well maintained but an extra one is needed near the rear amenities block. The pin code access and exit positions are less than friendly for other than cars drivers.
Dogs should not be allowed in caravan parks.
Great place to kick the shoes of well priced, great for kids to play .
Great location close to beach and town
Stayed with our caravan.  Dog friendly. Perfect location for walking along beach and into town.  Facilities great and reception staff lovely.  Easy check in and booking

I have stayed here another of times and I found it fun and enjoyable with alot of things to do. This place is great for the little ones to play and explore at. I have always gotten the ensuits and they have always been nice, tidy and clean to use without a hassle. I would 100% recommend this place to anyone even if it was just for a pop in.

We stayed here for 2 nights 22-25th april and we stayed in a park cabin. Very compact, idea for 2 adults and 1 child. Note- do not cook on hotplates  in cabin as smoke alarms go off and for a long time too. There is a camp kitchen close by. All i can say is lots for the kids to do here. Suituated just out of town although some shops are close by. Bed in cabin was comfortable. Couch for tall people is very low, hard to get off. 4 out of 5 for this accomadation.

Fantastic, really loved it. Always do.
Will keep going back over again.

3yrs ago
Great place to stay, my dog even enjoyed it, even though it was very busy, our stay was still great. Not far to travel to the dog park either and the weather was perfect.

14th of July 2025
Stayed here in a different area, with the larger ensuite with power, which was clean and tidy and very roomy.
Absolutely awesome location. We have stayed here quite a few times when we want to stay at Victor Harbor. My dog always enjoys her stay here and we have lots of places to walk outside of the caravan park. I have added fresh photos, as we stayed in a different area.
NRMA Corporation provides poor service(

Supposed to stay 5 nights with kids and grandies to enjoy a rare break. One of the grandkids acquired gastro and we had to leave after the second night. Asked if the park could honour the remaining three nights for another time and was told no! So disappointing! Had a 6 year old in tears and no compassion.
Great location! Heaps for the kids to do

The NRMA Caravan Park, Victor Harbor is well maintained, offers a variety of facilities (including a pool, water park, skate track), aptly equipped camp kitchens and large drive through sites for big vans. The staff are friendly, polite and professional. It is located on the foreshore with the Encounter Bike/Walk accessed by gates, and a
river on two sides. I recommend it and thank the Call to the Coast for the opportunity to enjoy a week at the park.

We had a great time at this park. It was clean, with lots of bins, and the toilet and shower block was sparkling. We felt safe and secure. We loved the drive through sites. The park was easy to locate, and it is right next to the beach and close to town facilities. We would go back there again.
Great caravan park, we went for Easter and staff put on activities and an Easter egg hunt for the kids. Will be back next year for sure.

We stayed one night in March 2026, travelling to Kangaroo Island.
NRMA Parks are always great, and this was further confirmation.
Our cabin was clean and neat.
We could park right next to the cabin, and could use extension lead to plug our fridge in overnight.
Yes, well recommended.
Thank you.
(We recommend the Victor Hotel for beers and a meal!).

Lovely park with great facilities.

Sites are spacious and staff work really hard to keep the place looking lovely.

Well worth a visit.

Wifi available but not the best

Two nights booked. Caravan park was adjacent to concert venue. Perfect! Rooms were clean, if sparce on crockery. Understandable I suppose by today's standards. Still, would come back as it was very convient to still through the town as well as accessing the Concert venue

An excellent place to stay
Good facilities and friendly staff
Only 200 metres down to a supermarket with excellent produce

An excellent location over looking the lawn area , close to amenities, seafront and pool.
The cabin was roomy and very clean and given access to the cabin early as it was ready for use. It was well equipped.
The staff were helpful and ready to assist.
It was excellent will return.  Cheers John
Our first trip out with our van, txt and email communication was fantastic and relevant. The en suite site we had was great and the bathroom was squeaky clean.

3 stars is for the cabin. We usually stay in a Seabreeze cabin and is lovely. Yes the 2 bed cabin is cheaper, but it doesn't mean the the handles to the cupboards and doors should have all the paint
peeling off or scratched off. Beds were very ridiculously soft and therefore, uncomfortable.
I'd come back to the park, but would never stay in that cabin.
All it takes is a coat of paint and change the kitchen cabinet handles.

Great place to stay.
You need to go there.

Clean, tidy and well maintained.  The staff was friendly and very helpful. The Studio Apartment that we stayed in was very clean and contained tea and coffee facilities,  a comfy big bed and a cosy well equipped bathroom.
For outside of the apartment cooking, there was a clean well equipped camp kitchen just a short distance from our door.
The park is very well situated close to the beach (walking distance) and close to town.
So much to enjoy at a very reasonable price.
No complaints from us.
Great Park. Staff are so helpful. Our family had a lovely time. All 3 Generations. 🥰
Only issue was dodgy internet connection in the studio cabin. Hard to watch a movie when it continually stopping
Top experience
Great place but no internet in our unit.

This park is large, with over 300 sites of varied types of accommodation. In addition to standard caravan powered sites, there are cabins, villas, studios and even Safari glamping tents. The parks main drawcard is its location - within metres of the beach front. This is easily accessible from the park and the popular Encounter Bikeway (Victor Harbor to Goolwa) runs past the park. The amenities are in need of refreshment and repair with many shower cubicles missing wall mounted shelves. The overall park is clean and tidy with many facilities for families & children. A swimming pool, pump track, bouncing pillow & playground are provided. Walking or bike riding to the centre of Victor Harbor is easy. The park is pet-friendly but all dogs must be leashed/contained. Communication with guests is enabled via mobile phone text messages. Entry & exit from the park requires keypad codes rather than the more modern automated vehicle registration camera check. This is a park I have used several times and despite its size have found it enjoyable. However in peak holiday periods the park is very crowded.

Great location - within walking distance of Victor Harbor township and the Granite Island Tram - along a shared bayside pathway. |Definitely camp/cabin accommodation for families with young children - with an expansive play area and water park.|The powered camp sites have fixed ground tarps.|The disappointment for us was the amenities. We were assigned a site to the west of the park and the amenities block closest to us was very tired. IMO the female bathroom bordered on being dirty. If we were to return, we would definitely book a site with a private ensuite.

Stayed here recently and had an absolute blast 🙌 staff are amazing friendly helpful very accomadating shout out to the reception lady who lives on Hindmarsh Island sorry I forgot your name you made our whole trip special ! Kiosk has coffee little ice creams etc DVDs , can hire go karts, pool had a shade sail very clean , jumping pillow splash park and water slides a big hit with the kids and us overgrown kids :) arcade is awsome ,and the bike/scooter track, we hired a couple of bicycles one evening and rode around the park with my little girl in the kids seat taking it all in , sun was setting and it was just a perfect way to end a fun day , beach access is great, plenty of bbqs around , our ocean breeze cabin had its own BBQ on the balcony facing the sea absolute beauty the mornings with a coffee and sunsets were incredible, cabin aircon was really good very tidy and clean spacious with built ins and fans in every room loved it ! Had everything u could think of , if I could criticise anything it would be that the playground is very small and old if that was updated this place would be perfect 👌 will definitely be back highly recommend!

Ok where to start.
Arrive onsite find someone 1/2 on our site. Ask him to move and we get. ‘ oh well all setup we are not moving.
Get shifted to an ensuite site but in morning have to shift. Not too happy about this so ask management what they can do. Winner winner chicken dinner Management say ‘how would you like a cabin for weekend. ‘ wow yes please 🙏
Great service and management of situations highly recommend ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️+
Well done guys. We will be back.

Stayed on a powered site. The amenities are kept very well. Large caravan park with large sites. Great facilities for families. Pool,splash park, water slide, games room and just a short stroll to the beach. Walking distance to Victor harbor's main shopping district. Will definitley be back.
Used our SA voucher. The park is beautiful & within walking distance to town centre. Everything about the 2days was fabulous. Will definitely be back again. Thankyou
Very friendly clean and sufficient great stay thankyou
Just a quick visit to see my sister who was staying there. A great family park. Will look at booking a longer visit.
Our powered site was right down the back in the dirt with no shade. Very disappointed.
Great park with great amenities. Office staff were friendly and helpful. Close to the beach, had beach access, and close to the shops etc.

Dog friendly cabins are extremely overpriced and I would not recommend them. The beds were old and uncomfortable, it hurt to lay on the uneven sunken surfaces. For the same price others get a cabin with nice kitchens, bigger bathrooms and new appliances + a beach view. The dog friendly cabin doesn't have any of that and lacks a dishwasher and working wifi.
Only positive was the location 😔

I know the site itself is good, however overcrowded which means facilities were dirty. People un-respectful next to us arrived late and having public scenes at 3 AM. After I have to get out of my place to go to the toilet they’ve had to leave. I understand that is not up to the camping business but if you like a  quiet and peaceful place you might ask to the staff the next to the river side and not in front of the beach.
Very nice park, walk into town along path or bluff. Big sites and quiet.
We would have stayed here but after trying to ring 7 times with no answer and waiting for return calls, we booked elsewhere.

Really great location, close to a big coles and victor harbour, really amazing facilities, loads of showers and toilets, super clean as well.

Big shout out and THANK YOU TO WAZ (Warren)!!! He absolutely saved our life when we were having car troubles, was really friendly and approachable and went out of his way to help us!  Honestly can't thank him enough, he is such a gem of a human being and you guys at NRMA are so soooo lucky to have him!!

We stayed here two nights on a powered site with an RV. Firstly the staff here are very friendly and do an amazing job to ensure you enjoy your stay. Ample very clean amenities. Walked along the beach into town for dinner. Many families with their kids who were having a great time. I highly recommend staying here.

The pet friendly cabin was in need of a complete upgrade, and the shower light had a cockroach in it. For the amount of money paid for our stay l would expect better accommodation.  Wifi does not work at all but is advertised, and when my wife inquiried about it at the office the staff really didn't seem to care. The location is the only drawcard to this park. The other cabins seem to look in better condition and are of a better standard. Pet owners really deserve better than this, and I wouldn't recommend this park until it had a complete overhaul of its pet friendly cabins. The photos on the  website must be at least 20 years old because that's definitely not the cabin we stayed in.
Great park, close to town and the beach. Nice clean amenities.

Proximity to Victor Harbour and Granite Island is great however during school holiday ( can’t comment on other times as haven’t been there then) the sites are super squeezy.  Access to Park, from Council roads, was probably great decades again however now it is super busy the sites are too small for big vans and lots of traffic   Kids ride their bikes are under the park, n the narrow roads, which is good great however dangerous it people parking / exiting vans.  Sites are just too small .  Fortunately we booked a drive through however still far too tight
Toller Campingplatz, sehr ordentlich und die Toiletten/Duschen waren sauber. Der Campingplatz ist direkt am Strand. Zu Fuss ist man in 30 Minuten im Dorf.
Why stay in a motel when you can have an excellent stay in a studio room at the NRMA caravan park here in victor harbour.  We will be back.

Big park!
Great park!
Excellent caravan park. Highly recommended. Plenty to do for the kids.

We stayed in an accessible safari tent. Tent was great and fully accessible for someone in a wheelchair. If you are in a chair, you would have to sit sideways at the sink as there is a cupboard underneath. Glassware and mugs are also not at an accessible height, so you would need to get the park to get them down to the bench prior to your stay. There is accessible parking right out the front of the tent with a EV charger if you have an EV like we do. The safaro tent had a split system air-conditioner and a faux fireplace. We requested some clean towels halfway through our stay and we returned to find that not only had our towels been replaced, but our bedding was also replaced, which was nice. The park also dropped off a large oscillating fan for a couple of the warmer days during our stay.

The park has a pool (unheated which is a little disappointing, especially when Victor is one of the colder areas in the state) there is also a bouncy pillow, splash park and waterslide, which the kids all love.

The park has multiple camp kitchens (no ovens). We had our own bathroom so we didn't use the parks shared facilities, so can't comment on them. There is also an entertainment room with games like air hockey for when the weather isn't great.

Staff were very friendly and accommodating. I recommend a stay here and we will be visiting again.
Great quite park, heaps for the kids to do, close to shops, just an awesome atmosphere
Sensational location. Very helpful and friendly staff. Clean park grounds and well maintained.

We tented on a powered site. The space for each site is very narrow and is quickly consumed by a tent, parked car and trailer. The amenities are kept clean and well maintained. The camp kitchens are limited and could be improved with more modern amenities. The water park, pump track and pool is great for young kids. The definite highlight of staying here though has been the proximity to Victor Harbour and the beach. It's an easy walk or bike ride into the town centre and the beach is a short walk over the dunes. Would be happy to return for holidays in the future.
Stay often here great location.

My recent stay at the NRMA Victor Harbour Beachfront Holiday Park was simply fantastic, and I genuinely mean it when I say the team here goes above and beyond for their guests. This park deserves every bit of a 10/10 rating!
From the moment we arrived, the staff were incredibly friendly and welcoming, setting a wonderful tone for our holiday. They were all smiles, helpful, and truly made us feel at home.
I want to give a special and heartfelt shout-out to Maria at the front desk. Maria is an absolute star! She was so friendly, professional, and went the extra mile to assist us with a couple of queries we had during our stay. Her cheerful attitude and willingness to help is a true credit to the park. Thank you, Maria, for making a great trip even better!
The location is stunning, the facilities are clean and well-maintained, but it's the exceptional customer service that really sets this park apart. If you're looking for a holiday park where the staff are genuinely friendly and dedicated to ensuring you have a great time, look no further.
Highly, highly recommend! We can't wait to come back!
Lovely spot to stay. I stayed alone, as a young female solo travelling in a van. I felt very safe and the facilities were great.

The family is staying at this park for the eigth year in a row. I just had to write a review about the service that we have been receiving from the manager. Being a group booking and having to make updates each year to the ever changing needs of six families Leo the manager has been absolutely wonderful in being customer focused. His patience and understanding in ensuring that our needs are met and fees correct has made for an anticipated enjoyable stay
Un excellent camping situé proche du bord de mer, avec de nombreuses installations modernes et récentes. Nous avions apprécié notre séjour, merci encore pour votre accueil!

It was a long walk to the beachfront from our van site, and the beach was behind a fence. The staff were lovely and the other guests and long time residents were great to talk to. Close to town. Nice park. Only downside was that there were little bits of rubbish everywhere.  The staff just need to spend some time doing an outside clean.
Friendly and helpful staff, beautiful sites, will stay again

Nicely set up park however pool wasn't heated as advertised so couldn't use it during our stay. Cabins starting to look a bit tired. Friendly grounds staff and great location. Would only visit again if there was a cheap deal on
Loved it.
Just stayed one night but it was lovely. Special mention to the amenities, how amazing were the showers! I'd drive 10 hours back there for those showers
This place was amazing especially for family my son didn't want to leave and go explore as he was having to much fun with other kids and so much to do at the park

This park has plenty of space for all kind of set ups, from camping, caravans, cabins and glamping safari tents.
We had an ensuite site, with plenty of space and our ensuite was beautifully clean and so convenient. It is also very child friendly with plenty of things for kids to do to entertain themselves. You can get some snacks and get your gas bottles filled also. Very close to all shops, restaurants, beach, walking/biking track and all attractions.  Overall we had a fantastic stay. We have already been here a few times before and would love to come back.
Thanks for our stay!

We visited with our granddaughter specifically to take advantage of the play equipment.  It also landed on the October long weekend.  The place was packed with children.  Not at all restful for us  but the kids seemed to be having a ball.
Stayed in a powered site for 5 nights. Great showers and toilets. Kids had fun in the play areas.

Really lovely site with great activities for kids and adults, inc heated pool, heated water slide and games room. Great site ensuite complete with towels and soap. Also very clean amenities. Really helpful and friendly staff who changed our site when we arrived when the national office had booked us a caravan site instead of tent. Give this place 5*. Lost star is for all NRMA websites only listing national 1800 phone number meaning you're dealing with people who don't know the venue.
Brilliant place to stay , throughly enjoyed it ! Highly recommend

a clean tidy staff very kind and caring atmosphere was good and warm beds where clean extremely tidy. and in a wonderful location they where lovely it's a kind placs..the water park was lots of fun my grandson's loved playing in it kept them fully entertained .they didn't want to leave they had such a great time
Staff are very helpful and attentive to guests needs. Well maintained park with excellent facilities.
Fantastic holiday park 10/10 for service awesome pool and slides check them out thanks NMRA Victor Harbour we will return soon cheers Rob and Di Smith
Cheap and great local restaurants.  Nice and clean.
Bed not readyParking is far
Had great time walking around this place. Didnt feel tired at all enjoying the breeze and views while walking.

Ideally situated to visit Victor Harbour and the Lower Fleurieu Peninsula. The park offers good sized caravan sites.
Ablutions are exceptionally clean. Overall, we can highly recommend staying at these park.

The rooms are average, missing a lot of basic amenities. Could use a table in the room. The wifi is the worst, terrible. I was here for work purposes and wasn't able to use the wifi of being so slow. Very expensive place for the price
Went there to visit a mate came to visit the town
Close to town good park for families

Location is stunning , the park is well situated in Victor Harbor with great pubs and eateries. Beds are very comfortable in cabins but cabins need to be updated , mainly floors as you can see all the vinly and carpet very worn . Apart from that we would happily stay there again
Very convenient and at heart of town
Nice place in general, had a great time  👍

Cabina studio pulita e ben accessoriata.
Cucina da campo con tutto il necessario, un ringraziamento speciale a Chris che ci ha dato la pentola per cuocere la pasta. Campeggio super consigliato!
I absolutely enjoyed my stay i wish could've stayed another night but definitely will be back!!

No other parks compare in the region, clean, tidy,  the team are all soo friendly and welcoming, they go above and beyond to make your stay the best it can be.  The sites are well kept and maintained. The beds in the  cabin we had are super comfy and plush. This park always is improving. Drive an electric car and found the charging stations in park  super confusing so did not use - only feedback is if the management could train the staff how to use or provide a  how to use card - but there is an RAA fast charger in town near woolworths. But not a detractor or negative as technology is ever changing. We book and visit 3 or more times a year see you in novmeber :)
Well placed to enjoy the town.
An large Caravan Park just a 10 minute walk to the centre of  Victor Harbour along The Esplanade
Beautiful spot, clean amenities, great showers, affordable, pet friendly, we camped in our van and the sites are large enough for a canopy, very happy with our stay
Comfortable accommodation, good facilities and location

My grandmother and myself stayed in the Accessibility parkview cabin. Very spacious and cosy. EV charger attached to cabin. Easy to move around and highly recommended for people with low mobility.

Only issue is the bathroom gets very wet and slippery, not enough drainage & shower curtains could be longer to prevent water going everywhere.

Value wise, considering I used my NRMA membership for a discount, I consider it decent. Around $800 for 5 nights, that is with discount.

Since the accessibility cabin doesn’t have its own washing machine we were required to use the onsite laundry, which is clean. Though a TV or some chairs would be really appreciated. Downside is all the machines (excluding the irons) require $1 coins. As someone who is 99% cashless this was a bit of a set back. (Please see attached photo for costs of laundry).

Overall if I come back to Victor Harbour I wouldn’t mind staying here again. Now that I know the quirks of the place I could work round it.
Couldn't fault this place.Everything is perfect

Great park with fantastic amenities. Absolutely worth having a ensuite site in peak periods. Some issues with drainage in the bathrooms and sites, but that could be down to the fact that it was stormy and raining the weekend we were there. Great central location, activities for families etc. Love that they had food vans and activities for the long weekend, made it even more special. Great service from park staff. We'll absolutely be back.

Nice size park with good amenities. Good looking waterpark and slide, bouncing pillow and plenty of bbqs. Our cabin (Ocean Breeze Villa) was a good size for the 4 of us and fitted out well. Modern look and feel, only negative was the shower was pitiful; poor flow and pressure (assume due to mineral build up from their bore water). Staff were very friendly and helpful. Would recommend, we’ll be back!
Excellent location. We had a cabin which was modern and comfortable.  A leisurely walk into town.
Absolutely enjoyed our stay very friendly staff clean amenities.highly recommend this park .thanks all for the great stay.

Stayed 4 nights in my campervan,lovely big powered site,very close to amenities and camp kitchen which was appreciated. Everything well looked after,bathrooms and toilets cleaned regularly,nice clean camp kitchen.All staff very friendly Highly recommend this caravan park

What an amazing park. Plenty of powered and unpowered sites, cabins, glamping accommodation and amenities. It's well set out and only a stone's throw from restaurants and cafes etc. Victor Harbour really does have everything you need on your trip. We stayed in an ensuite site and so glad we did. Clean. Modern. All the comforts of home. The only criticism I have is that given the size of this park, a few more washing machines and dryers would be greatly appreciated by all campers. Will definitely be back when travelling through next time.
Spent 4 nights here, plenty of room for our motorhome. You can walk into the CBD, Granitr Island, plus beach front views

The amenities blocks were dirty and had warning labels for asbestos. There is no family bathroom, just a corner bath shoved in a shower cubicle, which is awkward to use.
Had a staff member come and tell us our kids had to stop running around at 6.55pm because my sons feet were too loud (he didn't even have shoes on)
Another families children and adults were being loud until well past 9-10pm.
Location was great and kids loved the pool.
Nice place to be.I
We overnighted with a campervan. Good facilities with many clean toilets and showers. Friendly staff to welcome us after the reception was closed.
Caravan Park is in a good location. Amenities were clean but are in need of a good steam clean and/or an update.
Very warm welcome. Studio are very comfortable and staff is amazing. Cheers mates 🏄‍♂️🇫🇷🤙
Beautiful park but can be busy. New grassy sites are amazing. Clean facilities and lots of activities for kids.
Allowed me to drop my bag off early before checking in was appreciated
How amazing is SA - Victor Harbour 😍 a must stay people and the walk to Granite Island is perfect

Awesome place to stay with family.

Good, can you make a big deal for me? Would you like it?

Great customer service, neat and tidy ensuite site. Close to beach and town.
Will come back again.
Van sites are a tight. Staff are very friendly
Karavan in Sehr schlechten Zustand nicht zu empfehlen. Wir haben ihn nicht benutzt sind ab gereist

As much as I hate giving negative reviews this one has to be done. We arrived after booking a pet friendly cabin to find the cabin in the back part of the park they were run down and dirty. Put it mildly I would not let my dog stay in it. The bed wasn't made I was told do it yourself, the cooktop was beyond rusted away, the couch was beyond dirty the kitchen falling apart and the smell preferable stench of some kind of cleaning was over bearing. We can't fathom why some parks think its ok if you have pet to get the rundown version of a cabin? No one travelling with pets would live like that let alone pay to stay a night. It's degrading and demeaning to pet owners. We stay in wonderful places allowing pets and stayed in many Cabins and they offer new ones as well ....happy to pay extra. Please consider that pet owners are clean and responsible people give them some luxury. We will never be back and will not recommend you as a pet friendly stay.

We booked two adjacent safari tents to house a group of friends (6 people) travelling together. Overall we were glad we tried out these tents in the basis that we didn’t have anything but praise for their appearance, fit-out, comfort and kitchen supplies etc. It was nice to see champagne as well as wine glasses - thank you!!!|The aircon did struggle but that isn’t surprising since it was nearly 40 degrees - meaning the outdoor baths filled with cold water were very handy indeed whilst the main pool was absolutely jam packed. ||What disappointed us was the lack of seclusion and the fact that the car parks are not positioned directly next to each tent. The VERY lacking privacy quite frankly destroyed the whole experience.  And we don’t just mean the fact that your neighbouring safari tent guests can see straight into your outdoor baths, but rather that the whole tent area, that looks like it should be just for use by the safari tent guests, is actually a free-for-all and not separated. Not even a suggestive sign to say ‘safari tent guests only’….||It’s not meant to sound like an elitist comment but with all other similar glamping tents in other parks we have stayed at, the premium price paid has been commensurate with the separation away from the entire park’s customers. We had families who were not staying in the tents just roaming around outside them  gawking and in many cases extremely noisily messing around in the fire pit and pizza oven area, tearing around ripping up the gravel on scooters!! It seems the bbq there is considered communal, so not ‘reserved’ for the tent guests, presumably because each tent has its own bbq. Some guests even went up into our tent’s outdoor deck whilst we were out visiting our friends’ tent next door and there were kids were climbing over everything. The excuse by the mother when confronted was that she thought it was empty - well so what, these lovely tents shouldn’t be used as playgrounds by kids staying in other cabins or vans who feel like ‘exploring’!!||So now that we read this from their website we can only laugh…. ‘Nestled in a serene setting, they offer a peaceful retreat from the hustle and bustle of everyday life.’|It was NOT serene or peaceful in the slightest!

My partner and I are aged pensioners and was absolutely disgusted when the office girl chose to put us on a site within the permanent residents and alongside a van that I would describe as a dump. There was plenty of empty sites where other tourists were placed so my only explanation as to why she shoved us back there is because my partner paid in full when he booked over the phone. I have taken many photos to back up my complaint. We went back into the office immediately and i told the office girl that I will be writing a scathing report on trip adviser and was then moved to one of the best sites in the park. It shouldn’t have taken a complaint to put us on a lovely site. We didn’t expect the best site but a reasonable site without being worried about mice and rats running around.  We don’t have an issue with permanent residents as my partner lives permanently in a caravan park. At $45.00 per night for a powered site you expect a nice site. So in future we will not pay in full when booking  in  and will check the site before paying in full. As we stayed From Friday until Monday I think the Office girl thought she would shove us back there incase they get an influx on tourists booking in over the weekend. And of course we had already paid in full and couldn’t get a refund. ||Another thing to be aware of is that the water in the first shower runs into the second shower. I was in the first shower and noticed my shampoo was running into the next shower drain. There is a purpose built large gap between the showers allowing water from each shower to run into each other.||We will never stay in this park again and would not recommend it to anyone ||If that’s how that disrespect their patrons. ||There is a beautiful Big 4 Caravan Park only a few kilometres.
Good caravan park close to town 👌   will stay again 👍

We had a wonderful stay here for 5 nights everything was in good shape and amenities were well cleaned
Glenn
Honestly, one of the best NRMA's, amazing room, good facilities, only downside was the "heated" pool was ice cold but was still refreshing.
Great spot, clean and quiet
Beautiful place, amenities were in great condition and everything is clean. Already booked a time to come back!

Great location and good amenities that are regularly cleaned. Enjoyed the few days spent here camping in our tent. The highlight was the pool and water slide during the 30+ degree day!! Also loved the weekly events like bbq night and morning coffee truck

Will never stay at your caravan park again. Firstly you say beach front, you cannot see the beach at all. We were placed down the back of the park near the annuals which wasn't very attractive, when needed to go to toilets at night there was no lighting, it was very dark. Had gum trees around us and after windy night there was quite a mess and nobody came around to clean up fallen branches. Your amenities were clean but that's about all your park had going for it. Will not stay there again.

Fantastic stay at Victor Harbor Beachfront Caravan Park!

We  stayed at Victor Harbor Caravan Park on the 18th and 19th Feb 2025 and it was an absolutely wonderful experience! The park is in a perfect location, just a short walk from the beach and close to all the attractions Victor Harbor has to offer.

The facilities were spotless, with well-maintained amenities, clean bathrooms, and great camp kitchens. The park is pet friendly. We were travelling with our 13 year old pug and she was made to feel very welcome.

The sites were spacious, and the park had a welcoming, relaxed atmosphere.

Whether you're in a caravan, camping, or staying in one of their cabins, this park is an excellent choice. We had a fantastic time and will definitely be back. Highly recommend it for families, couples, or anyone looking for a relaxing coastal getaway!

The location is fantastic!
We actually liked this caravan park despite the effort to fit as many people and gear into it on rather small sites.
To me this is the definition of overcrowded, and it was very difficult to get our 33 foot van into a 36 foot site and have room to put our awing down and park the ute.
The park was packed with Aussie day campers!
They did offer to move us, after setting up and realising we had no room, and I was unsure if it would have been any better, so stayed put.
The park amenities were ok and the pool looked like Bondi Beach on a good day, with standing and peeing room only!
I reckon it would be OK on any other normal weekend.

Love this place, it holds very special memories for our family and this was our 7th visit. We stayed in a seaview cabin, will never get sick of this view. Just beautiful! So relaxing. Great size for a family( there were 5 of us), only small things to suggest would be a Platter in the kitchen for the awesome platters while sitting on our verandah and some scissors. Unfortunately the waterslide wasn't working for most of our stay but did get fixed on our last day so we made the most of it but the pool got well used! Pool temperature was great, even on the few cooler days we were there. All the staff were lovely and helpful and looking forward to our next visit, thanks again!

Pool is NOT heated. It was freezing and we couldn't swim for almost our entire visit after paying premiums to have access to a heated pool in uncertain weather.||||The grounds were lovely and the location was great so if you don't care about the pool you'll probably have a good time, but for me, I felt really let down because I'd paid specifically for a place with a heated pool as that is our idea of a perfect December holiday. ||||The floor was also not sufficiently cleaned so our feet got black from walking on it, and there were some other issues, but other than that the cabins were nice and well stocked. The main negative thing was the pool.

Great location and good facilities. Could use some more kitchen space and sinks but adequate. Decent amenities. Some of the Powered sites on the beach side of the park have beautiful shade under Norfolk palms (I think that's what they were).
Kids can go wild in the water park and pool area. A shade sail would keep the bouncy pillow from getting super hot in the sun, but during our stay it was ok. Perhaps Victor doesn't get as ridiculously hot as elsewhere and it's not as necessary but seems like it would make for a more sun smart play area. Overall, I would visit again in a heartbeat!

Firstly we were slugged $465 for one night which we thought was very steep. We thought we were getting an Oceanview cabin and the rooms looked really nice in the photos on the website.

When we arrived, we were told that we didn’t have a car space next to our cabin but in an allocated car park not near the cabin we had to then lug all our luggage to the cabin which wasn’t great with two kids. Then when we arrived at our cabin we realised it was not an Oceanview cabin. I wouldn’t consider a gap between two cabins an ocean view.

When we entered our cabin it did not look worth $465. It had an overwhelming smell of fragrance. The tiles in the toilet had no grout. The bathroom had black mould and the drain smelt horrendous. In the kids rooms there was popcorn kernels all over the floor so clearly wasn’t vacuumed before us.
The outdoor dining set was covered in bird poo which had been there for awhile and the outdoor barbecue didn’t work.
It wasn’t a pleasant stay.
Victor harbour a historical place to watch . The sea looks so beautiful.
Checkin online makes it easier when you arrive. Sites reasonable size. We are at the far end of the park, quieter. Bike path into town is great.

Grosser Platz mit gutem Angebot für Kinder, schöner Poolanlage und das Städtchen Victor Harbor sowie Granite Island ist zu Fuss erreichbar, was wirklich nicht selbstverständlich ist in Australien. Zudem direkter Strandzugang. Die sanitären Anlagen sind in Ordnung.
I stayed in my Swag, but it was an extremely nice stay.
Clean and fresh rooms plenty of things for the kids to do great location clean amenities a must stay if in the area for holidays or just a quit few days with your partner. A solid 9/10
Beautiful spot. The kids love it too. Very friendly staff and great atmosphere

We stayed a week here over New Years and thoroughly enjoyed our stay given how busy it was in peak season. Our drive through caravan site was spacious with lovely grass surrounding. Amenities blocks not far away which were kept clean. Plenty of safe play spaces for the kids with a scooter pump track, jumping pillows, mini splash park and a pool. The camp kitchen looked great. Petrol station just down the road along with a grocery store. The main CBD is just a short drive or a nice walk which was alive and pumping with plenty of tourists. Will be back for sure!
Safari tent and surrounding area was lovely

The location of the park was good however, the park itself was average. The staff were rude and very unhelpful. The site wasn’t marked out very clearly and when I asked for assistance the staff was very rude and unhelpful. There was some big tent set up by a religious group who kept knocking on our caravan door handing out flyers which was unpleasant. We actually checked out a day early.
Excellent spot for caravan and cabin stays.

I am a regular at this caravan park and stayed here last week. I was surprised with the $6.15 charge for swiping my card to pay for my stay. Was not advised of this upon booking but this is the first time that I have been charged this amount.  Most places charge $2.00 - 3.00 dollars. I think this is unnecessary as to be honest I would have rather paid extra 10 dollars on top of my bill and not know about it. I watched on current affair how banks have slugged customers and the banks have turned away from doing it as it isnt right. I do find this move arrogant and I am sure I’m not the only one that has been charged.
Since I have read you replies I would appreciate that you don’t as they have been repetitive in answering “how you strive” etc etc so no need. I just believe we can be and do better. Other than that place is great thanks.

Great location with beach access.  It's not the best swim beach, though. However, a few meters down towards Victor Harbor, there is a stunning swim beach with beautiful views.
The campsites are spacious and the ground staff are very helpful and friendly.
Toilets are clean and ample.
Cooking facilities are clean and ample.
Just wish they had more shade at the camp sites but still worth a five-star from me and our experience here.

It was our first visit and we really enjoyed the ensuite caravan site. Very good shower and bathroom. Well maintained lawns and facilities overall. We found the roads could do with a more frequent clean up, specially before the weekends. That will improve the overall aspect of the park. Definitely a place to visit many times again.
Just a thought about the entry/exit gate. We found very difficult to enter the PIN on the keypad that is positioned very low and on an angle, making the entry/exit with the caravan and large vehicles very difficult.
Great time spent with grand kids around the water park. Corellas need another cull

Nice Park sadly let down by the roads being covered in Gum nuts,  bark and leaves making it look untidy.  Nice pool, waterslide, and water play park.  Clean and tidy camp kitchen, amenities and games area.

Sadly, I had a horrible experience staying here last night (Saturday night) 👎🏼😭😭 My husband and were in a tent, on unpowered site 291. The noise level was very high for many hours through the night. Music, very loud talking with constant swearing.. a chain smoker too! A fellow camper on campsite 292 had the same experience 😭😭 Both of us had paid to stay 2 nights, but left after 1 night. The nightly rate is very high, not good value given what we've paid in 100's of other caravan parks. I would not recommend this caravan park if you are into nature, wildlife, peace and quiet ⛔⛺⛱️🌊 The majority of unpowered sites are very exposed and not shaded. It's sad that this park attracts visitors who are disrespectful of neighbouring campers. It might be worth booking during the week if you are very keen to stay here! It may be quieter?
The staff I spoke with over the phone, including the lady who checked us in were all lovely and considerate 🙏🏼🌼

Just spent three nights.  Lovely grassy and spacious  sites but a few things need attention. No powerpoints in amenities block? So strange. This is a basic requirement for me. Also, pathways to access amenities very limited necessitating walking through sites which I really dont like to do. Lack of drainage points for sink run off too. Again, not sure why this is? For such a large park a really pokey undersized office space. Staff were under the pump, didn’t offer any local information and were keen to get rid of us. Weird. If you have that many sites to manage provide a reception space for your staff to deal with the clients.

Room in Studio cabin was well thought out and clean. Comfortable bed and terrific shower. Fan over the bed was wonderful! Everything was within easy walking distance. There happened to be a band playing live music and singing! It was wonderful! As was the Gin and Vodka and Rum tasting! Loved every minute. My
grandkids loved the pool,

jumping pillow and the water park! Camp kitchen was terrific, well maintained as was the landscaping! Will definitely be back! Thankyou to the staff there! You made our stay very enjoyable!

We recently had the pleasure of staying at the NRMA Victor Harbor Beachfront Holiday Park for two nights, and it was an absolute delight! Our park view cabin not only offered a lovely glimpse of the sea but was also impeccably tidy and well-maintained.

From the moment we arrived, the reception team welcomed us with warmth and enthusiasm, providing extensive information about the local area that truly enhanced our stay. Their hospitality set the tone for our entire visit.

The park itself is one of the nicest we’ve encountered, with a fantastic atmosphere that makes you feel right at home. We were particularly impressed by the thoughtful layout and the numerous amenities available. We’ve heard great things about the kids' activities and are already planning to return during the school holidays to take advantage of everything on offer.

Overall, our experience at NRMA Victor Harbor was outstanding, and we can’t recommend it enough. We’re looking forward to our next visit!

Great campsite. The pitch was spacious enough, the facilities were nice and clean. Hot water was very welcome. Reception lady was very welcoming and did a great job checking us in. Nice and close to the beach.
Clean ensuite facilities. Privacy great for a big Park. Helpful staff.

Great place if you have kids, may be a bit too busy if you don't have them in peak times, plus you will pay a premium for kids entertainment regardless of whether you have them or not.  I was disappointed as we took electric scooters (we are in our 40's) which we ride with helmets respectively when sightseeing.  We were told they were not permitted, yet there were cyclists riding much faster all over the roads with no helmets on & no lights at night.  There were kids riding trikes which were lower than a car wheel & were very dangerous as they could not be seen very well.  It just seemed double-standards.  We always take our scooters as they are easy to get around & take another car off the road in an already clogged area.  If a cyclist can ride a bike, I can't see why scooters can't be used.  We will stay elsewhere next time for that reason alone, this is our first experience of a park not allowing them.  Can't rate the amenities, as we used our own.  Sites were level & well sized, noting you can only have one vehicle per site.

I stayed here for 1 night and was glad it was only for one night as the room was dirty and smelt like pets. I accidentally booked a pet friendly cabin but would have expected it to still be cleaned and aired out before the next guest comes in. I love pets but when I paid  $214 per night, I expect a better room. The bed was not even made for the double decker bed for the kids. The main bed was nicely made. The blanket for the kids were dusty that my son who was sensitive to dusk kept sneezing. We ended up with no blanket for the night. There was no linen to cover the blanket in the kids room too.
There was stains on the kitchen bench and the sofa was dirty too.

Overall not a good experience. For the price I paid, I should have gone to a nice golf resort in Normanville and just drive 30mins to Victor harbour for a day trip.

On a positive note, the girl at reception was very nice and listened to my feedback as I was leaving.
Beautiful caravan park.  Big sites.  1 . 5 klms into town if walking.

Faultless. We have stayed in a variety of accommodation at this park from budget cabin, ocean view cabin, powered sites, ensuite sites and my favourite the Safari tents twice, and everything is always perfection.

But the tents... The tents are incredible. So comfortable, everything is well thought out and spotlessly clean. The outdoor area is class. Can't wait to come back again!
Keep doing what you're doing guys, the grounds are fabulous and every staff member is lovely.
Not great at looking after long time customers. Changes to the park made a lot of people loose a lot of money. And there lie about fire compliance. What a joke

Great location with access to a wonderful bike path
Well done
Beautiful park. Well maintained and great location
Could not have been more happy with location. Service from staff & cabins were fantastic.

Want to share our positive experience staying for two nights in NRMA camping.

We arrived lately after closure of reception as we failed to book any stay in advance. I’ve made a call to the facility manager Chris and he kindly arrived to check us in even though the office was closed for an hour or so.

Chris was very friendly and helped us to plan our route around the coat and even download and register NRMA app so I was able to get a member discount.

The site was clean and well maintained, however Christopher was the person who made our stay such a great experience, wish him all the best.
The best holiday park in Victor Harbor
One of the best holiday park.

Checked in for two nights, but had to leave after one, the power was not working from our site.  Once we got home, everything was ok , so definitely the site.  Let the park know the next day, but have not heard back from them. The facilities were clean and tidy.  But extra service would have been nice.  It's not what you do, it's how you handle it that's important.
Cheap for beach front
Very good park lots of romm and we had an ensuite wich was great.

Beach Front Luxury Camping "Glamping" was a unique experience! The safari tent with two singles was well-equipped and cozy, offering heating and air-conditioning for comfort. The full bathroom and lounge area with a TV and fireplace added a touch of luxury. The outdoor area, featuring a private barbecue and bath, was perfect for relaxing while enjoying glimpses of the ocean. Highly recommended for a unique getaway!

Excellent park and very close to town. There are some excellent bike tracks which are cool for our e-bikes. Amenities okay and the men’s toilets has a swollen jamb at the mo, so very good not having to input the code. Staff great and send you texts with updates etc which is great. They gave us a discount without the need to ask. 100% happy and we’ll be back for sure. 10 out of 10 from us!

Really great place to stay
Very quiet..we stayed in a studio villa very clean... will return..
We are camping here in our rv. The location is the best for staying in Victor harbour. Right on the beach and walking distance to all the pubs and shops

What's the perfect location holiday stay without a "great team" of professional, dedicated, friendly & helpful staff.
From beautifully maintained grounds to meticulous house keeping  to an office with the most friendly, helpful & welcoming staff.   Thankyou to you all & my special thanks to Cass.
Most definately will be spreading the word of this fantastically run beachfront holiday park at Victor Harbor NRMA.

Was very much looking forward to staying here for the first time recently however some sad and unfortunate circumstances happened unexpectedly and we could not make it happen   We had to cancel at very late notice and therefor I did not expect a refund nor did I ask as per terms and conditions. However the park was very gracious in giving an almost full refund and I am very appreciative. Will definitely be booking again in the future   Thank you very much
Excellent park, close to town and lovely walks. We’ll be back

This park is awesome, there so much for the kids to do, I love the walking track around the Inman river and so close to the beach, staff are friendly and helpful, grounds are kept clean, amenities are very clean, rooms are clean and some of the views from the cabins is great an easy walk to town centre,this place is a great base   for exploring the area so many beaches and great town to check out in the area, we will be back

we are a family of 5 and have stayed here multiple times and love the location and all the great activities that the park organise for kids and adults.  Staff are very friendly and helpful. Amenities and cabins are clean and we'll equipped.
Days before this visit, due to an unexpected event, and had to change our accomodation type and the Staff went above and beyond to help us. They were just amazing. Highly recommended!!!
Great caravan camping spot. Lots of glamping and cabin options. Friendly staff plus lots of activities for all ages.
Had a great night away for my sons birthday, wonderful staff and a beautiful location. Would definitely recommend :D

We recently stayed in the ocean breeze cabins, they are comfortable and well maintained, with a great view.
Staff were so helpful and friendly.
Ammenities and kids areas were nice too. We’ll be coming back again :)

What a great park. Friendly staff who are very helpful. Spotless cabin, great verandah with a spotless BBQ as well. On the beach front, shops very close and Victor Harbour itself is absolutely beautiful. Loved our 3 night stay here. Thanks guys.
We had a 2 night stay in one of the lovely safari tents. The customer service, facilities and location are excellent and I would highly recommend to anyone looking to stay in the area.

I had a great getaway in the van park in a most lovely clean roomy cabin. Everything was so clean and well presented.My two day stay felt a lot longer as it was so quiet and peaceful there. I could see the sea from my cabin so nice. The children in the play area had the best time. I can recommend this park as it’s close to town and it is very well presented. Will return again soon. Jenny McRae

Wonderful well maintained grounds, lots of amenities lik pool and laundry facilites, lots of space. We stayed in a 2bed cabin and had everything we needed and loads of space in it so could unpack a bit and spread out.
Staff were great! Friendly and helpful
Location is close enough to the centre to walk along the coast or a quick minutes drive in, a IGA, fuel station and burger joint in immedoate proximity. Would recommend!
Beautiful, well maintained, very clean, comfortable, great position. Superb staff and amenities.

Excellent park set right on the beach.
Staff were professional and friendly.
It got quite windy at night but that did not take away from the experience.

Friendly staff, cleanest amenities I've seen at a van park. Great games rooms (love skill testers) bouncing pillow and water park was a great hit with the little one.

Highly recommended.

I recently stayed at this beautiful and clean park, and it was an absolute delight! The safari tents were incredible and provided a unique and memorable experience. I haven't seen anything like them anywhere else. Plus, the location couldn't be better – close to town, with easy beach access and shops within walking distance. The housekeeping team does an exceptional job, ensuring that the cabins and amenities are immaculately looked after. The reception team was incredibly helpful, providing insightful information about the area, including maps and local favorites. They even gave us tips on where to possibly spot whales during the upcoming season! I can't wait to return for another stay. Highly recommend it to anyone looking for a fantastic getaway!
$35 for unpowered site. Felt abit unsafe leaving gear with a open gate next to our site with the public walking past. No water and wifi was terrible. Nice place though if you have a caravan
Love this caravan park close to beach and a nice walk into the main shopping area or to one of the restaurants.Park staff are very friendly and helpful.

We stay here every year at Easter for good reason. Great for the kids to safely explore and play and great for us adults to relax. Staff are super helpful and friendly and the amenities are clean and well maintained (Even when the park is packed at Easter!). The pool was also very clean with zero complaints from our kids, staff certainly take pride in maintaining this area. Highly recommend this park!

First and foremost the park is situated in an awesome part of the country and well worth a drive.|The Park is clean and tidy - shower/toilets were easily accessible and well kept.|The park is dog friendly - which is awesome for travelling pet owners.|The heated swimming pool is also very good ||At the back of the park there gate access to a bike/walking track and beach area - just awesome||The reason I could only offer the average rating is due to the sites (drive through sites). As a caravan owner a drive through site is awesome, however as with many parks - the drawback is the poor cramped sites. Love thy neighbour only goes so far an extra foot on site width makes for a much more enjoyable stay!  The sites have a mat for awning which is excellent but then as with most caravan parks grass sites are rapidly disappearing - not only is this visually disappointing walking on stones and dirt where grass should be also detracts from enjoying the park.||Overall if your just passing through it has good  facilities but if you’re after that relaxing visually inviting site for a period of time to sit back and relax with Friends I couldn’t recommend it.
Great park with excellent amenities. The bike paths are very easy to access from the park. Close to town and the water front. Very quiet too.
Had a great stay at the park. The park manager Leo and his team were amazing at sorting out any questions I had. Will definitely be staying here in the future.  Highly recommended.

A great place for the kids to have fun. It’s also great to catch up with friends have a bbq and a walk near the beach. They also promote movie nights sometimes which is great fun.
Victor Harbor is awesome and the location of the park being walk distance from town helps to have a meal and a drink avoiding driving and spending money in taxi.

Stayed in a caravan so we didn’t get to try a room. The location is walkable into town and the main thoroughfare. There’s a bike track the runs along the beachfront into town and that’s really good. Service is regular on the parks amenities and it has nice facilities for cooking and entertainment. Only issue is that they have no built in oven or cooktop in their camp kitchens and the only real cooking surface is the barbecue. Beautiful spot and close to everything.

This is a mixed review, so please read if wanting to stay there. |Its a really good park. It's easy to access, the amenities were clean and there were a bunch of facilities for the kids to use. The sites were a good size (but more on this later).| In summer, the beach access gates would be extremely handy. However in April, it was extremely cold and there is nothing to act as a wind break. Some nights I did not sleep due to the strength of the winds and the concern for our awning, gazebos etc.|Based on these parts, I would have stayed there again. However mid trip we had a slight issue due to another camper and because of the way it was handled, we will not consider staying their again.|Whilst staying at the park, we noticed that the marked lines did not match with the fake turf sections. We followed the white lines are the actual site boundaries and gave the best proportion of parking, to van, to awning space. However somewhere in the middle of our 8 night stay (covering 4 sites and 18 people), a person came along onto the site next to us. He placed his awning out, extending the straps to the extent of the fake turf. This meant that our ute was unable to be parked in our allocated spot, well within our marked white lines. I politely knocked on his door to kindly ask if he could please move them and that my husband was happy to assist. He refused the help and moved the straps. The next day, an employee from NRMA Victor Harbor came down and looked at the white line markings. He soon returned with an angle grinder and a white spray can. I asked if there was an issue and he replied their was but we can all be friendly neighbours. I explained I was, but clearly its stirred the pot and our neighbour was unhappy. The employee proceeded to adjust the white line to the edge of the fake turf, meaning our ute could no longer easily fit between their site and our van. He mentioned that the whole section of sites along our road were going to be altered, but when we offered to move my brother in laws car so that he could continue completing them, he declined. This gave us all the impression that he was only completing the one, to please the man who complained, instead of continuing and listening to the problem it now created with parking. The man pulled his awning down that day and stayed a second night without it out, before leaving. We still had several nights remaining on what was now a smaller site with not enough space for a vehicle to park. All four of our sites were not impressed with the way it was handled, basically to "shut up" the guy who complained, instead of explaining to him that there was an error on their end and that the lines were the actual markings for the sites. This has left a bad taste in our mouths and we will not be returning to NRMA Victor Harbor.
Awesome location walk down the street
Excellent location. Close to the beach and a short walk into the CBD. Well maintained grounds and clean, modern amenities. The best hot shower I have ever had in a caravan park.
All good, except the heating. The room was below comfortable temperature and the single air conditioner was not sufficient.

We spent 4 nights here in a powered tent site. Yes a bit of a hike to the amenities block but do-able. Walking/bike path to town and tourist spots,but found it just as easy to walk along the  main road pathway. I agree with a previous review about smoking, we had a chain smoker in the next site and had  constant odour into our area.
Nice and quiet after hours. We travelled in school holidays so be warned , kids everywhere. Camp kitchen well set out, and with shade  but in the rain, seating  is wet.
Overall a very good family friendly and safe park with access to tourist spots. Thankyou to park staff for tidy and clean environments.
A large and really good caravan park. Well maintained. The water park is great for kids.

Just spent the Easter weekend at this place and have had multiple incidents with staff That are rude when trying to sort out an incident where another parent has physically and aggressively grabbed someone else’s child and all the staff had to say was basically in other words that it’s not there issue nor problem

This place is ment to be a family fun place to get away and would highly recommend not to come here when the staff can be so blank and heartless towards a young girl who was grabed by a total stranger  piss poor effort by staff

Great park lovely staff fabulous position we've been here now quite often short walk to main street cafes etc only 1km. Clean amenities lovely pool and kids pool . Lots of lawned sites and unpowered/powered. Only 1 hr from Adelaide in the Fleurieu peninsula

Gee what a fabulous caravan park. The owner operators and service staff should be very proud. The sites are serviced with very clean sites with power and water. The ammenities are second to none. Gee whiz there is a lot to enjoy and to explore in Victor Harbor. I found the foreshore walking tails are fabulous. I’ve shared a few foreshore photos.

We have just spent a really lovely, albeit hot long weekend at the park. Location is perfect, walking distance to granite island along the foreshore. The staff were amazing, the amenities kept clean even thought the park was full.  Thoroughly enjoyed the pool, water park and water slide with the kids and grandkids. Camp kitchens were so clean making them pleasant to use. Each had very nice BBQ s and table and chairs if you wanted to eat over there. Lots of trees for shade. Will definitely use again

The Rooms are Lovely.
Very clean , But little room to move around, the Bed would have given more room if turned around.  Kept tripping on the Quilt Cover getting in or out of Bed , bit of a Safety issue.
Cabins not great

Good location  near city centre and has a local supermarket close by.
Short walk to very scenic beach front
Cycle and walking path cuts across the front with access via a gate.

Lots of EV charging points are being commissioned.
Great park.  Very busy but love it
Great for caravaners

It isn't a hotel! It's a stretch of park with parking, toilets and BBq facilities and a nice lawned area next to the river, where people can go and gather together, for picnics, whatever the accession!
It is near a Caravan Park!?
We go there once a year for a Réunion gathering of people who like to come together and reminisce and tell their stories of times passed by, we're not getting any younger, so our memories serve us well of those time's.
Have no photo's!

We made a last minute booking and were very warmly greeted, even had our gas bottled filled and dropped to our site by Chris, who then gave us some great tips of where to fish. Amenities were very clean including the laundry with clean machines and a nice iron (Not that I used it 🤣).
One of the lovely staff who was finishing work for the day kindly gave us a lift into town as we were having trouble getting a taxi.
Thank you
Great spot next to the beach and walking distance to town

The pool is fantastic and the dep end has a ledge and at you can touch the size. The warerpatk is great also, although not too fast. The games room is great and has an air hockey table and skill tester machines (bring your coins). The camp kitchen is very clean and has 4 tables and shade screens on the pergola, and 2 BBQs and a large fridge.
Clean, quiet, and friendly staff

Fantastic! Stayed in a cabin and this place was fantastic, rooms are spacious and well insulated, while the park itself is kid heaven with the inflatable, mini pool, and movies nights on saturdays. Staff are pleasant and friendly. Park has rear gate that opens onto the bike path, and is only a 5min walk into the town centre.
This place is super great 👌 we enjoyed our stay! We will definitely come back. The room was so clean and the amenities are great especially for us who had a very active 2 year old. 😊😊😊

Great park,everything for the kids you could wish for.Very clean,great amenities.The cabins are very spacious and immaculately clean.Would be nice if the larger cabins had ovens.It is next to the water,so you get great onshore breezes.If you need a great place to stay with your family whilst holidaying this is the place for you, wether you have a caravan or camper or just need a cabin,you can be accommodated.
I love this holiday park. The staff are amazing and the accomadation is great too. Always my first choice.

I have stayed in the two bed cabin's 2 years in a row and both times next to people that chain smoke. I don't think this should be allowed in the parks when there are children every where. The smell comes straight into the room and it stinks so we could not have our window or door open at all. Also the pool needs to be bigger as always busy and only enough room for all the kids. Plus more areas with shade to sit.

Caravan sites are mostly roomy with the occasional tight fit. Great location with bikeway at front esplanade of the park. Five minutes walking distance to central Victor and Sunday markets. Centrally located for exploring the region. Would like to see some walkways to the amenities from the front of the park though. Seems a little weird walking through campsites to get there.
Great location and service , wide sites close to the beach and. town centre everyone said we would like it and we do

Minimum 7 night stay is tough going in a tent, but other than that the place is excellent for kids with constant activities and pool time. The DJ on NYE was a great way to spend the evening, and the location to town is excellent. Clean facilities and felt very safe letting the kids roam around.

Hundreds of daily 5.30am galahs do not make for a relaxing sleep-in holiday. The 5gb per day of advertised Wi-Fi is only available to the select few that are lucky enough to be located close to the admin building otherwise.........nothing. Good gimmick though to get the suckers in then not provide the service. All accommodation  places that jack up prices during holiday season should be investigated and fined for price gouging, they are charging, if not double, then near on double to normal prices without providing any additional services. Won't be back.
Had a great stay , cabin was quite new , modern , clean and comfortable, plenty of things for children to do at the park. Staff were friendly had a great stay and will definitely be back

Had high expectations for this place, but was disappointed. Seaview cabin, so their top priced accommodation, in peak season. Cabin not cleaned properly - dirt, sand and dust under couch; dirt and pill (medication) under couch cushion. Bathroom and wardrobe doors don't close properly. Visitors walk across the front and sides of your cabin and look straight in. You can also hear every word of their conversations, the cabins are that close to each other, meaning no privacy. Cars cannot be parked beside your cabin; they are not even within view. Can only see the tv properly in the master bedroom from one side of the bed. 1 ply toilet paper given, and not enough for a 4 night stay. Internet didn't work; unsure if we were given the wrong password (tried numerous times) or if the signal can't reach this far. Spare blanket smelt like someone had urinated on it. Nowhere to hang wet clothing and towels. A clothes line across the balcony or bathroom, or a clothes horse supplied would have made a huge difference. Pool is like a backyard pool and always busy. No bins provided for paper, cardboard, plastic containers, and glass (glass jars) recycling. One positive is that it is a reasonable walking distance into town.
Wouldn't stay here again.

Kathy on the phone was the most wonderful person ever . She even called us back and helped us get a longer stay . We are still hoping we will not have to move on Boxing Day . This park is lovely and well kept . Perfect location and just a small walk into town . Has a pool and lots of kid things to do . Definitely would stay here again
Good for all, family's and old people.

Pleasant surrounds, varying level of abultion block standard, main block easily accessed, gr8 hot water flow, pity shower doors removed. Maintenance is an ongoing issue for any c/park given the public attitudes these days.
Reasonably level lawned sites.
Staff very obliging.
Current modern & multi configured cabin investment should enhance patronage.
Easy access with pleasant 400 m walk to community library/cafè & main street businesses.
Foreshore walks adjoining the park.
Only stayed one night but was a lovely little room in the king bed studio. Staff were friendly and helpful. A nice little weekend away :)

Three blokes, one two room cabin, two nights, friendly staff, quiet park.
Enjoyable stay. It is a bit of a walk into town, but that will do you good.
Great location, walking distance to township and local services. Drive through caravan sites. Friendly staff. Clean facilities. Plenty of activities for kids to stay entertained.
Great van park and cabins. Awesome for kids.

Had a Beautiful Stay in a studio cabin even tho wasn't very spacious had a lovely comfy bed, Beautiful shower, We could sign into our Netflix Beautiful walks behind the Caravan Park, Friendly reception

Fantastic staff and grounds with awesome amenities.
However it would have been nice if our cabin had the beds made and if quilts were available.
Lovely place to stay with pool, water slides and jumping pillow for the kids to play.

Great facilities, and close to everything. You can walk from here to the causeway and Granite Island. Do the E-Bike hire... especially from the boys at Victor Harbour Bicycle Shop. Gotta try the wood fired pizzas (and Authentic North Indian Curries) at the Beach House Cafe (complete with compact and bijou bar), yeaaahhh!
Had a great time. Enjoyed my stay.
Great spot, walk to street. Kids have enough to do. Pool could be bigger and a bigger playground would be good. Pump track is great with water park. Friendly staff

Very clean and tidy park with friendly reception.  We stayed in a safari tent and found it very comfortable with everything we needed.  Could hear the ocean and enjoyed the sun on the deck in the morning!

My favourite place to stay when I come down to visit family all over Goolwa and Victor. Seriously very neat, clean and the park has so much to offer! Friendly staff and great play areas for the kiddies to be outdoors! Love it here. I'm hoping to get out there on new years!
Great place to stay,had a powered site, the service we received from the start was excellent,  park was well presented,  handy to the shops,  we hardly used our car ,
Eight on the beach loved the location, staff were friendly and helpful
We've stayed here twice now and will comeback its very central to everything and is a lovely park.
Great location and very clean amenities, will be back again thankyou

This was a great place to stay, friendly staff and was set in a wonderful location.||The cabin we stayed in was very modern, clean and comfortable. We had a large combined kitchen, dining and living area, two bedroom rooms and bathroom. ||We we lucky enough to have water glimpses from the front verandah and living room. ||There were laundry facilities in the park which was handy and a swimming pool which in winter we didn’t use. ||The park was not far from local shops and a short drive to pubs and various restaurants and takeaways. ||We would recommend staying at this establishment when in Victor Harbor.

Running behind schedule we were contacted by the park to organize an afterhours check-in which we had forgotten. That started out trip on a positive note, arrived after dark go find a clear map with our site pointed out.
Healthy green grass which is amazing, the amenities are clean and have what you need. We tried the pool, which were not sure if it's heated but it wasn't freezing cold when the temps outside are 14'c.
The park is relatively busy, yet we are away from most people which is also great
Enjoying our stay very much and will be coming back for sure 😍
Nice park in a great location
Bought a powered site. Have caravan will travel.😁
A good park to stay at.
Excellent price for a studio cabin which was very clean and cosy. Friendly, helpful staff. Would stay again.
Great location. So many options of accommodation. Lovely and close to the beach and to walk into the main part of town.
Beautiful park. Clean facilities and lovely staff. This town is beautiful
